dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used price_pos_20 to represent standard deviation bands (0.025 ≈ 1.5 SD below 20-period mean; 0.975 ≈ 1.5 SD above). Mapped 'narrative intensity' and 'contrarian sentiment >55th percentile' to RSI extremes (RSI>55 for bullish overextension, RSI<45 for bearish). Vol_ratio_20 used for volume spike detection. Exit conditions: 1-2% profit mapped to 1% threshold with returns indicator; time-based 5-bar hold enforced via hold_days_max. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to support both long and short entries based on overextension direction.

backtest_rule.reason: Used price_pos_20 to represent standard deviation bands (0.025 ≈ 1.5 SD below 20-period mean; 0.975 ≈ 1.5 SD above). Mapped 'narrative intensity' and 'contrarian sentiment >55th percentile' to RSI extremes (RSI>55 for bullish overextension, RSI<45 for bearish). Vol_ratio_20 used for volume spike detection. Exit conditions: 1-2% profit mapped to 1% threshold with returns indicator; time-based 5-bar hold enforced via hold_days_max. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to support both long and short entries based on overextension direction.

source_rule_text: Traders should recognize that investor overconfidence and overoptimism cause overreaction to information, pushing prices too far and creating mean-reversion opportunities; discipline requires restraint from joining the crowd narrative and instead positioning for price correction.
